#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Iran Conflict Update — Mar 30, 2026

Days 30-31 of the Iran-US/Israel conflict. IDF struck the Tabriz Petrochemical Company sending Brent crude toward ~$115/bbl; Iranian missiles and drones struck ALBA aluminium plant in Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium in Abu Dhabi (8 total workers injured), marking Iran's new targeting of Gulf industrial infrastructure beyond oil. An Iranian missile hit a chemical plant at Ne'ot Hovav in southern Israel, raising hazmat concerns. Trump told the Financial Times the US may 'take Kharg Island,' while USS Tripoli arrived with 3,500 Marines. CENTCOM confirmed Iran's missile launch rate is down 90% and drone rate down 83% from Day 1, with 393 total attack waves recorded. Analysts issued 'April oil cliff' warnings as global SPR buffers approach exhaustion by mid-April. The April 6 Hormuz deadline remains firm as a four-nation Islamabad summit pursued diplomatic off-ramps.

Sections updated: kpis, casualties, econ, mapLines, mapPoints, claims, political, assets, retaliation, strikeTargets, events, meta

Mencho/CJNG Update — Mar 30, 2026

Day 36 of the post-Mencho succession crisis: O3 continues consolidating CJNG leadership under a collective council structure as the sixth week of the succession vacuum closes. Operativo Enjambre has cumulatively produced dozens of CJNG-linked official arrests across Jalisco and Michoacán since February 22 — including Tequila's mayor (March 29) — systematically dismantling the cartel's municipal-level protection networks. Argentina's formal FTO designation of CJNG (March 26) is now in effect, making it the third country after the United States and Canada to apply such a designation. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary high-value targeting priority with a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty.

Sections updated: events, kpis, meta

Sinaloa Fragmentation Update — Mar 30, 2026

El Mayo Zambada's defense sentencing memorandum is due today (March 30) with his Brooklyn sentencing set for April 13, 2026 — he faces mandatory life in prison and a $15 billion forfeiture. A Sinaloa Cartel affiliate who smuggled drugs into Georgia was sentenced to over 15 years on March 27, underscoring the cartel's continued US distribution reach despite the ongoing Culiacán civil war. The 'Days of Open Civil War' KPI was updated to 567+ days since the September 9, 2024 outbreak, and the site day count is now 614 days since El Mayo's capture. No confirmed major armed clashes or territorial changes were documented in Sinaloa for the March 23–30 window beyond the ongoing La Mayiza ~90% territorial control status.

Sections updated: events, kpis, meta

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Taiwan Strait Tensions Update — Mar 30, 2026

PLA reached a 19-sortie single-day peak on March 29 — the highest count of the late-March escalation spike — with 13 sorties crossing the median line across all four ADIZ sectors simultaneously and 11 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan. This completed a three-day surge (6 → 15 → 19 sorties) that represents the sharpest week-over-week acceleration since March 25. AIT Director Raymond Greene publicly backed Taiwan's NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget on March 29 and called for 5% GDP defense spending by 2030, the most direct US endorsement of the full bill yet. Taiwan's army also conducted combat readiness drills on the Matsu islands and announced expanded missile output for the T-Dome air defense system. KPI, map line, and claims sections were updated to reflect the latest sortie peak and the AIT Director's public pressure on Taiwan's legislature.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, kpis, claims, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/taiwan-conflict/

Trump Presidencies Update — Mar 30, 2026

Trump claimed Iran had agreed to 'most' of his 15-point peace plan via Pakistani intermediaries on March 29, while simultaneously floating the option of seizing Kharg Island — which handles 90% of Iran's oil exports. Iran's foreign minister continued to deny direct negotiations and submitted a counterproposal. TSA officers received their first paychecks under Trump's executive order on March 30 as the DHS partial shutdown entered its 45th day — the longest partial shutdown in US history — with Congress on a two-week recess and no deal in sight. US equity markets posted their fifth consecutive weekly decline, with the S&P 500 down 7% year-to-date and the Nasdaq entering correction territory, as recession probability models rose to 49% (Moody's). The March nonfarm payrolls report was released on Good Friday while markets were closed, with investor reaction deferred to Monday.

Sections updated: events, kpis, econ, political, meta

#OSINT #ConflictTracking #IntelDashboard #Watchboard

Ukraine War Update — Mar 30, 2026

Russia launched 442 drones and 1 missile overnight March 28–29 — the largest single-night barrage of the week — killing 4 across Ukraine including strikes on Odesa, Kryvyi Rih, Poltava, and Ozernoye Airbase; Ukraine's air defenses shot down 380 UAVs. Russian forces captured Kovsharovka in Kharkiv Oblast (pop. ~17,960), with approximately 800 Ukrainian soldiers reportedly encircled in the Kupyansk salient, while 236 combat engagements were recorded — the highest in recent weeks. Ukraine struck Russia's Ust-Luga Baltic port for a fourth consecutive night and destroyed 3 Russian Tornado-S MLRS systems. Russia announced a temporary gasoline export ban effective April 1 in response to Ukrainian strikes on refineries and ports, while Mediazona/BBC confirmed a milestone 206,202 Russian military deaths by name. US Secretary Rubio confirmed Washington communicated Russian ceasefire conditions to Zelensky, with the diplomatic gap remaining wide.

Sections updated: events, mapLines, mapPoints, kpis, casualties, econ, political, assets, meta

World Cup 2026 Update — Mar 30, 2026

Amnesty International's 'Humanity Must Win' report warned that the World Cup risks becoming a 'stage for repression,' citing mass deportations and ICE enforcement near stadiums — FIFA has still not secured any enforcement moratorium with 73 days to kickoff. Iraq's squad completed an overland journey via Jordan to arrive in Monterrey for Tuesday's final vs Bolivia, after regional airspace closures caused by the ongoing US-Iran conflict. Italy braced for a hostile atmosphere in Zenica amid the Dimarco controversy ahead of the Bosnia-Herzegovina final. The USMNT held final pre-match training for their March 31 Portugal friendly — the last test before Pochettino's May 26 roster announcement — with Portugal missing Ronaldo, Leão, and Diogo Costa.

Sections updated: events, kpis, timeline, claims, political, meta

Dashboard: https://watchboard.dev/world-cup-2026/
